steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The claim is that Winnemucca creates suspense by emphasizing her physical response to an event. The correct quotation must do two things: (1) describe a physical, bodily reaction — not just actions or observations — and (2) generate a feeling of suspense or tension. A racing heart, trembling, or fearful bodily sensation in response to an uncertain threat would satisfy both criteria.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — "So ended our feast, and every family went to its own home in the pine-nut mountains, and remained there till the pine-nuts were ripe. They ripen about the last of June.": narrates seasonal activities with no physical response and no suspense. ✗
  • Choice B — "Oh, how my heart jumped when I heard a noise close by. It was a horse running towards us. We had to lie down close to the ground. It came close to us and stopped. Oh, how my heart beat! I thought whoever it was would hear my heart beat.": centers entirely on a physical response — the narrator's leaping, pounding heart — to an unknown threat, creating clear suspense. ✓
  • Choice C — "The day we were to start we partook of the first gathering of food for that summer. So that morning everybody prayed, and sang songs, and danced, and ate before starting.": describes communal activities before a journey; no individual physical response and no suspense. ✗
  • Choice D — "Late in that fall, there came news that my grandfather was on his way home. Then my father took a great many of his men and went to meet his father, and there came back a runner, saying, that all our people must come together.": reports family and community events; no physical bodily reaction and no suspenseful uncertainty. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
